description_long: This helmet is a classical, full-coverage headpiece reminiscent of ancient Greek or Spartan designs, featuring a pronounced brow ridge and a T-shaped opening for the eyes and mouth. The surface is metallic with a dark, weathered patina, showing patches of lighter gold where the finish has worn, giving it an aged, battle-worn appearance. The helmet is smooth and rounded at the top, tapering down to cover the sides and back of the head, with a pointed projection at the center of the brow. There are no visible ventilation holes, decorative motifs, or modern attachments. The interior is not visible, but the helmet appears hollow and designed to fit snugly over a human head. The overall construction suggests it is made primarily of metal, likely bronze or a similar alloy, with a solid, protective build.
description: A weathered, metallic ancient-style helmet with a dark patina and a T-shaped face opening, featuring a smooth, rounded dome and no visible decorations.
description_view_0: Front view shows the T-shaped face opening, pronounced brow, and pointed projection at the center.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the helmet's smooth, rounded contour and the way it covers the side of the head.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the rounded, seamless back with no openings or decorations.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the helmet's continuous curve and coverage.
